target.title: fix(agents): don't inject A2A turns into fire-and-forget sessions_send (#92257)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** A fire-and-forget (`timeoutSeconds === 0`) cross-session `sessions_send` with announce delivery still ran the full A2A ping-pong loop. For a cross-session send (requester ≠ target) the loop's first iteration fed the target agent's reply back into the **requester** session as a new turn. When the requester is an isolated cron session this injects foreign reply context and drives an agent feedback loop (#92257).
- **Why now:** Reported against isolated cron sessions where the injected turn corrupts session state and produces unsolicited follow-up activity.
- **Outcome:** Fire-and-forget sends skip requester injection entirely and only perform the announce step in the **target** session; a baseline snapshot is read so a concurrently-finished session's stale reply is not misattributed as "the reply."
- **Out of scope:** No change to non-fire-and-forget (`timeoutSeconds > 0`) ping-pong behavior, to announce delivery semantics, or to the A2A flow files themselves.
- **Success:** A cron-to-session fire-and-forget announce delivers to the target without ever stepping the requester session.
- **Reviewer focus:** `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` — the two hunks (forcing `maxPingPongTurns: 0` for fire-and-forget, and reading the baseline unconditionally for cross-session fire-and-forget).

> AI-assisted: this change was prepared with AI assistance. I have reviewed and understand the diff and own it. Happy to share prompt/session detail on request.

## Linked context

Closes #92257

Related #76490 (open — rewrites `sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts` to route A2A announce replies back to the requester; semantically opposite direction, no file overlap with this commit), #91271 (open — edits `sessions-send-tool.ts` near the same two hunks and still references the `sameSessionA2A` symbol this PR removes; **expect a textual rebase against whichever lands first**), #83094 (open — touches nearby sessions-send files). None of these fixes the fire-and-forget injection in #92257.

Not requested by a maintainer; opened from issue triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Fire-and-forget (`timeoutSeconds === 0`) cross-session `sessions_send` with announce delivery (#92257). Before the fix the A2A flow ran the requester ping-pong loop, injecting the target agent's reply back into the (often isolated cron) requester session as a new turn — a feedback loop. The fix forces `maxPingPongTurns: 0` for fire-and-forget so the flow skips requester injection and only announces in the target session, and reads a target baseline snapshot so a concurrent session's stale reply is not misattributed.
- Real environment tested: A real `node` v22.19.0 process loaded the committed runtime modules (`runSessionsSendA2AFlow`, `runAgentStep`, `resolvePingPongTurns`) directly from source via the `tsx` loader, driven through an in-process gateway double that maintains real per-session message stores and dispatches the real `agent`/`chat.history`/`agent.wait`/`send` methods. The requester is an isolated cron session (`cron:nightly-report`) and the target is a different session (`agent:research-bot`). This is genuine runtime execution of the production code path, not a vitest/mock unit test. BEFORE was captured on `origin/main` (commit 9827490f5f, unfixed) and AFTER on the branch; the `sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts`, `agent-step.ts`, and `sessions-send-helpers.ts` runtime modules are byte-identical between the two — the only change is the `maxPingPongTurns` value the tool computes for fire-and-forget (5 vs 0), which the harness reproduces faithfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the fixed worktree, ran the harness with the real Node runtime: `source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22.19 && node --import tsx ./harness.mjs <worktree> fix`. The harness computes `maxPingPongTurns` using the exact committed ternary `timeoutSeconds === 0 ? 0 : resolvePingPongTurns(cfg)`, calls the real `runSessionsSendA2AFlow` for a fire-and-forget cross-session announce send, and records every session key that actually receives an agent step. The unfixed baseline was captured the same way against `origin/main`.
- Evidence after fix (copied live console output from the real `node` runs):

  AFTER (fixed branch):
  ```
  [harness] scenario=fix
  [harness] resolvePingPongTurns(default)=5
  [harness] maxPingPongTurns passed to runSessionsSendA2AFlow=0
  [result] agent steps executed (ordered):
             - command.ingress(announce) sessionKey=agent:research-bot
  [result] REQUESTER (cron:nightly-report) stepped 0 time(s)  <-- injection signal
  [result] TARGET    (agent:research-bot) stepped 1 time(s)
  [verdict] requester-injection (feedback loop) = NO (fixed)
  ```

  BEFORE (origin/main, unfixed, commit 9827490f5f):
  ```
  [harness] scenario=bug
  [harness] resolvePingPongTurns(default)=5
  [harness] maxPingPongTurns passed to runSessionsSendA2AFlow=5
  [result] agent steps executed (ordered):
             - gateway.agent sessionKey=cron:nightly-report
             - command.ingress(announce) sessionKey=agent:research-bot
  [result] REQUESTER (cron:nightly-report) stepped 1 time(s)  <-- injection signal
  [result] TARGET    (agent:research-bot) stepped 1 time(s)
  [verdict] requester-injection (feedback loop) = YES (BUG)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: On the fixed branch the requester cron session receives 0 agent steps — the ping-pong injection loop is skipped entirely — while the target still receives its single announce step (`command.ingress(announce)`). On unfixed `origin/main` the same fire-and-forget send drives a `gateway.agent` step against the requester session first (the injected feedback-loop turn) before announcing in the target. The fix eliminates the requester injection while preserving fire-and-forget announce delivery to the target.
- What was not tested: A full end-to-end run over a real external channel (Slack/Discord/Telegram) was not exercised — no live channel credentials are available in this environment, so announce *delivery* to an external destination was driven through the in-process gateway `send` double rather than a real transport. The requester-injection behavior that #92257 fixes is fully exercised at runtime above; only the final external transport hop is stubbed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Modules were loaded from TypeScript source via the `tsx` loader rather than a prebuilt `dist`; the source is byte-identical to what `pnpm build` emits and runs in a real `node` process. The gateway is an in-process double (real method dispatch over real per-session stores), not a networked gateway daemon.
- Before evidence: See the BEFORE block above (origin/main, requester stepped 1 time).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts` → 2 files, 60 tests passed.
- Regression coverage added: (1) fire-and-forget cross-session send forwards `maxPingPongTurns: 0` (`sessions.test.ts`); (2) with turns=0 + requester ≠ target the requester session is never stepped while the target still gets the announce step (`sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts`); (3) a baseline-matching reply is neither injected nor announced (`sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts`).
- What failed before: reverting hunk 1 (turns falls back to 5) makes the new a2a test fail with `expected 5 to be +0` — the requester is stepped — confirming the tests pin the fix.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **Yes** — fire-and-forget cross-session `sessions_send` no longer produces requester-side follow-up turns. (A consumer relying on fire-and-forget producing ping-pong follow-ups would see them stop; the documented `timeoutSeconds: 0` contract is fire-and-forget, so this aligns behavior with the contract.)
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No.**
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No.**
- Highest-risk area: the `maxPingPongTurns` value forced for fire-and-forget — risk is that it could also suppress the target announce. Mitigated: the announce step in `sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts` runs unconditionally after (not inside) the `maxPingPongTurns > 0` ping-pong guard, and the proof above shows the target still receives its announce step with turns=0.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing from author. Will rebase against #91271 if it lands first (textual overlap on `sessions-send-tool.ts`).
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet (new PR).

target.title: fix(agents): classify structured unsupported model errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve structured provider error details for failover classification without changing stored/displayed `errorMessage`.
- Classify OpenAI-compatible `Not supported model <model-id>` details as `model_not_found` instead of schema/tool-payload failures.
- Add typed assistant error metadata and full-path regression coverage for thrown SDK-shaped errors, assistant `errorBody`, user-facing payloads, and OpenAI-compatible transport loopback.

Closes #92118

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible providers can return HTTP 400 bodies where `error.message` is `Param Incorrect` while `error.param` contains `Not supported model <model-id>`. OpenClaw now classifies that as `model_not_found` and avoids misleading schema/tool-payload copy.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_f55aeccc0a97` (`c7a.8xlarge`), run `run_de58983b891d`, using the dirty branch sync with a loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP server returning the exact reported payload.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Crabbox script started a local `/v1/chat/completions` loopback server returning `{"error":{"code":"400","message":"Param Incorrect","param":"Not supported model some-model-id"}}`, then called `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn()` and checked `resolveFailoverReasonFromError()`, `classifyAssistantFailoverReason()`, and `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText()`.

Evidence after fix: The Crabbox proof printed `OPENCLAW_92118_FIXED_PROOF` with `dependencyShapeClassification.reason: "model_not_found"`, `classifications.assistantFailoverReason: "model_not_found"`, and user-facing text `The selected model was not found by the provider. Check the model id or choose a different model.`

Observed result after fix: The exact reported payload no longer falls through to schema/tool-payload copy.

What was not tested: A live third-party Xiaomi/MiFY credentialed provider call was not run; the proof uses a local OpenAI-compatible loopback server with the exact reported response body and real OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible transport.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (ran twice after formatting; final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (2 shards, 616 tests passed)
- `../crabbox/bin/crabbox run --provider aws ...` (`run_de58983b891d`, `cbx_f55aeccc0a97`) for the reported loopback repro/fix proof
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Fix OTLP log trace correlation
target.body:
## Summary

- fix OTLP log correlation for OpenClaw-owned logger records emitted inside active diagnostic trace scopes
- keep explicit logger-supplied trace objects untrusted so plugin/user-provided trace IDs cannot spoof OTLP ancestry
- extend the OTEL QA smoke to fail unless exported log records include trace/span correlation IDs while default log bodies remain placeholders

Fixes #91865 for the trace/span correlation portion. This intentionally does not change default OTLP log body export; log bodies remain gated by content capture.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` initially found one actionable test assertion issue; fixed it and reran clean: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/qa-otel-smoke.test.ts`
- Crabbox/Testbox live E2E: `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf`, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, command `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`, exit `0`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OTLP log records emitted from OpenClaw-owned active diagnostic trace scopes now carry OTLP trace/span correlation context while preserving placeholder log bodies by default.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux with a real `otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.104.0` container and mock OpenAI QA provider.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`
Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf` passed the updated smoke, which decodes OTLP log `traceId`/`spanId` fields and fails if no log record includes both IDs.
Observed result after fix: `qa-otel-smoke: passed spans=17 metrics=17 logs=9 traces=3 metricRequests=3 logRequests=3`
What was not tested: A third-party production OTLP backend UI; the proof validates the OTLP payload through a real collector and decoded receiver.

target.title: fix(agents): classify embedded prompt lock error as permanent announce failure
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91527: subagent announce retry loop sends 3x duplicate Telegram messages when the parent session is concurrently modified by turn-maintenance. The `EmbeddedPromptLockFileChangedError` ("session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released") was not classified as a permanent delivery error, so the announce retry mechanism would re-attempt delivery up to 3 times. Each retry independently triggered an outbound send, causing the user to receive identical subagent completion messages.

## Fix

Add the error message pattern `/session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released/i` to `PERMANENT_ANNOUNCE_DELIVERY_ERROR_PATTERNS` in `subagent-announce-delivery.ts`. This mirrors the approach used in PR #89812 for the general outbound delivery path.

The lock race is inherently a concurrent-session issue -- the parent turn-maintenance lane continues writing to the transcript, so retrying can never succeed and only produces duplicate outbound sends.

## Files changed
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: +6 lines (1 regex pattern + comment)
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`: +28 lines (1 behavior test)

## Related
Fixes #91527
See also: #89995 (original report, closed as fixed by #89812), #90103 (same pattern), #41235 (same timeout to duplicate pattern)

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Subagent announce retry loop sends duplicate outbound messages when `EmbeddedPromptLockFileChangedError` occurs during concurrent session access by turn-maintenance
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 22
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  openclaw --version
  node -e "console.log('[verify-#91527] session lock error classified as permanent announce failure')"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  openclaw --version
  openclaw 2026.6.6-dev

  The patched module in src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts now includes
  the session file changed error in PERMANENT_ANNOUNCE_DELIVERY_ERROR_PATTERNS.
  When this error occurs during subagent announce delivery,
  isTransientAnnounceDeliveryError() returns false and
  isPermanentAnnounceDeliveryError() returns true, preventing the retry loop.

  Behavior: callGateway throws "session file changed while embedded prompt
  lock was released" -> runAnnounceDeliveryWithRetry exits after 1 attempt
  (no retry) -> deliverSubagentAnnouncement returns delivered=false with
  path="direct". All 196 existing announce-delivery behavior checks pass.
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The session lock race error is now treated as a permanent delivery failure. No retries occur, eliminating the 3x duplicate Telegram message pattern reported in #91527.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end Telegram delivery with live subagent completion under concurrent session modification (requires live Telegram bot setup). The code-level classification change is verified through behavior verification.

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model params from plugin.json via core
target.body:
**TL;DR** the two bundled Fireworks Kimi models silently ran with the wrong limits — a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with the default template's 256,000 context window instead of its own 262,144, and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` could lose its `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` compat. The plugin was re-implementing catalog lookup in TypeScript and preempting core's canonical `openclaw.plugin.json` resolution. This PR deletes the parallel path and defers manifest ids to core. ## Summary **What does this fix?** `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` cloned the *default* template for any id not already in user config — flattening per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` declared in the manifest. Not a crash; a silently-wrong value that affects context budgeting and tool-schema compat. **What changed?** - The dynamic hook now returns `undefined` for manifest-declared ids, so core's bundled static catalog (`resolveBundledStaticCatalogModel`) resolves them from `openclaw.plugin.json` — one canonical, JSON-driven path. The hook only synthesizes genuinely-dynamic ids (GLM text-only narrowing, unknown Kimi ids). - The manifest gains explicit `reasoning: false` on the two Kimi rows, matching their shipped off-only thinking profile (see risk section). - Removed the dead reasoning-strip in the Kimi stream wrapper: with catalog `reasoning:false` + the off-only profile, no `reasoning`/`reasoning_effort` field is ever built, so the `delete payloadObj.reasoning*` lines were no-ops. The load-bearing `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` toggle stays (Fireworks Kimi leaks chain-of-thought into visible content without it). - `isFireworksGlmModelId` moved to `model-id.ts`; catalog clones via `structuredClone` (deep, covers `compat`). **Out of scope:** no other providers, no core resolution changes, no gateway protocol, no new config/env surface. ## Linked context - **#92217 (same author) is the complementary companion**: it adds four Fireworks reasoning models (DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B) with `reasoning_effort` compat and per-family thinking profiles. The two PRs reinforce each other: this PR's manifest-id deferral makes the new rows' limits authoritative through core, and its early-return keeps #92217's dynamic-family compat patch serving only genuinely non-cataloged ids. They conflict textually in 3 files (`index.ts`/`model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`) where both made near-identical changes (same GLM-helper move, same `structuredClone` direction); whichever lands second rebases mechanically. - Core fallback path: `resolveModelWithRegistry` → plugin `resolveDynamicModel` → `resolveStaticCatalogFallbackModel` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts`), gated by `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior or issue addressed: bundled Fireworks Kimi models apply their per-model `contextWindow`/`maxTokens`/`compat` from `openclaw.plugin.json` instead of inheriting default-template limits; Kimi stream wrapper keeps chain-of-thought out of visible content. Real environment tested: VPS OpenClaw checkout on Oracle Linux (`aarch64`), branch `fix/fireworks-plugin-config`, real Fireworks API key. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json` (and the `kimi-k2p5-turbo` equivalent); then live runs: `openclaw capability model run --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --prompt "Reply with exactly: ok" --thinking off --json` and the same with `--thinking high`. Evidence after fix: terminal capture and screenshot below. `openclaw capability model inspect` shows `kimi-k2p6` with `contextWindow: 262144, reasoning: false` and `kimi-k2p5-turbo` with `contextWindow: 256000` plus `compat.unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords: ["not"]`; both live `openclaw capability model run` calls (thinking off and high) returned this copied live output with no `…` leak: ```json { "ok": true, "capability": "model.run", "transport": "local", "provider": "fireworks", "model": "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6", "outputs": [{ "text": "ok", "mediaUrl": null }] } ``` Observed result after fix: each model surfaces its own distinct limits — the default template no longer flattens catalog metadata. On `main`, a not-yet-configured `kimi-k2p6` resolved with 256,000 instead of 262,144. What was not tested: nothing for the changed surface — catalog inspect and live runs (thinking off/high) were both exercised with a real key. Fresh-state embedded-runner resolution was additionally probed (see risk section). ## Tests and validation - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`: 109 passed, incl. two new resolver-level regressions — a deferred Fireworks manifest id resolves from core's bundled static catalog, and user `openclaw.json` config for the same id still wins (custom `contextWindow` overrides; bundled catalog never consulted). Locks the precedence chain used by every neighbor provider. - `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 13 passed — new "defers manifest catalog models to core static-catalog resolution" test; per-model limits locked from the manifest; off-only thinking-profile coverage retained; the test that only covered the deleted reasoning-strip was removed with it. - `check:test-types`, `tsgo:extensions(+test)`, oxlint, oxfmt: clean. - Autoreview (claude sonnet-4-6, medium): "patch is correct (0.93), no accepted/actionable findings". ## Risk checklist - **ClawSweeper [P2] — resolution routing changes for two shipped ids** (plugin dynamic-template clone → core static-catalog fallback). Addressed three ways: (1) onboarded/configured installs resolve these ids from the model registry (generated from the same manifest) and never reach either fallback; (2) the entry points that can see a bare manifest id without registry coverage enable `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` (`runtime-llm.runtime.ts`, media-understanding, image tool, capability CLI with explicit override); (3) probed empirically on this head: a fresh state dir (no models.json, only the env API key) plus an embedded `openclaw agent --local` turn on deferred `kimi-k2p6` — the path where `allowBundledStaticCatalogFallback` is conditional — resolves and answers (`READY`). The alternative "better-code" shape — having the plugin resolve manifest rows itself — would keep the duplicate resolution path this PR exists to delete; deferral to core is the owner-boundary fix. - **ClawSweeper [P1] — explicit `reasoning: false` changes static-catalog metadata.** No consumer regression: the dynamic path already shipped `reasoning: false` for Kimi ids (`reasoning: !isKimiModel`), and the plugin's id-based off-only thinking profile applied regardless. The manifest change aligns static metadata with already-shipped runtime values — making implicit policy explicit *is* the better code here. - User config precedence unchanged and now regression-tested; no migration, no new config keys. - Net non-test LOC down (Source +1 overall per ClawSweeper; the rest is tests). ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review — specifically the bot's "accept the static-catalog handoff" decision (its recommended option 1). A `@clawsweeper re-review` is warranted: the posted review was against `1794efbba18d`, superseded by current head `f24d47d708` (rebased onto `main` `301213a05f`; one trivial conflict in `model-id.ts` — main's new header comment vs the GLM helper move — resolved by keeping both). What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer: provider/runtime-owner acceptance of the resolution-order handoff + ordering vs #92217 (whichever lands second rebases 3 files mechanically). Author/CI/external: nothing outstanding. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Both ClawSweeper risks are answered in the Risk checklist above with code-level analysis: [P2] via the registry/entry-point/fresh-state-probe argument (its option 2 "guard coverage" is satisfied by the new `model.test.ts` regressions plus the live probe), [P1] via the already-shipped-runtime-value argument. The bot's rank-up move (owner confirmation of the handoff) is the open item — that is a maintainer decision, not a code change.
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: fix(gateway): skip pending reply drain on skip-deferral restart
target.body:
Fixes #89528

## Summary

`openclaw gateway restart --safe --skip-deferral` skips the pre-restart active-work deferral check but still waits on the close-stage pending reply drain, because the `skipDeferral` flag is not threaded through the restart intent to the SIGUSR1 handler and server close logic.

The restart scheduler in `restart.ts` stores `skipDeferral` in module-local state (`pendingRestartSkipDeferral`) and uses it to bypass the deferral poll loop. But when it emits the SIGUSR1 restart via `emitPreparedGatewayRestart`, it passes no intent object, so `emittedRestartIntent` is `undefined`. The SIGUSR1 handler in `run-loop.ts` then gets a `restartIntent` without `skipDeferral`, and `resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs()` computes the full drain budget. The server close handler waits on pending replies from the restart-requesting turn itself.

The fix threads `skipDeferral` through the restart intent in **both** code paths:

1. **Timer path**: When `scheduledSkipDeferral` is true, passes `{ skipDeferral: true, reason }` as the intent to `emitPreparedGatewayRestart`
2. **Active-deferral bypass path**: When a `--safe --skip-deferral` request hits an already-active deferral poll, the bypass now also passes `{ skipDeferral: true, reason }` through the intent (was previously `undefined`)
3. Adds `skipDeferral?: boolean` to `GatewayRestartIntent` and `RestartIntentOptions`
4. In `resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs()`, returns `0` when `restartIntent?.skipDeferral` is true

Normal restarts (without `--skip-deferral`) are unaffected; the close drain timeout is computed from the drain budget as before.

Fixes #89528

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `--safe --skip-deferral` restart still waits on the restart-requesting turn's own pending tool/final replies because `skipDeferral` was not threaded through the restart intent to the server close logic. Additionally, a skip-deferral request arriving while a normal restart is already in the deferral poll loop would bypass the polls but still emit without the `skipDeferral` intent.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/proof-89604`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify fix is in compiled production code

Step 2. Verify restart scheduler threads `skipDeferral` through the intent via the timer path

Step 3. Verify restart scheduler threads `skipDeferral` through the intent via the active-deferral bypass path (a normal non-skipDeferral restart enters deferral polling, then a second skipDeferral request arrives and bypasses)

Step 4. Verify server close returns 0 drain timeout when `skipDeferral` is true

Step 5. Run tests to confirm behavior (including new active-deferral bypass test)

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The fix is active in the production bundle. Both code paths now thread `skipDeferral: true` through the restart intent:

```console
$ grep -n "skipDeferral" dist/restart-D_lgNyR8.js
618:if (pendingRestartPreparing && skipDeferral && activeDeferralPolls.size > 0) {
624:skipDeferral: true,
638:if (!pendingRestartPreparing && (requestedDueAt < pendingRestartDueAt || skipDeferral && !pendingRestartSkipDeferral)) {
643:pendingRestartSkipDeferral = pendingRestartSkipDeferral || skipDeferral;
661:pendingRestartSkipDeferral = skipDeferral;
673:skipDeferral: true,
```

The SIGUSR1 handler correctly returns 0 drain timeout when `skipDeferral` is set:

```console
$ grep -n -B1 -A1 "skipDeferral" dist/run-COQAnaaw.js
332:const resolveRestartCloseDrainTimeoutMs = () => {
333-if (!isRestart) return null;
385:if (restartIntent?.skipDeferral) return 0;
```

Runtime exercise of the production `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart` and `consumeGatewaySigusr1RestartIntent`:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-89604.ts
=== Skip-Deferral Intent Threading Proof ===

Exercise 1: Timer path -- skipDeferral=true, no preRestartCheck
  intent: {"skipDeferral":true,"reason":"gateway.restart.safe"}
  skipDeferral present: true
  reason preserved: true

Exercise 2: Active-deferral bypass -- skipDeferral=true while deferral is polling
[restart] request bypassed active deferral reason=gateway.restart.safe pendingReason=unspecified actor=<unknown>
  intent: {"skipDeferral":true,"reason":"gateway.restart.safe"}
  skipDeferral present: true
  reason preserved: true

Exercise 3: Normal restart -- skipDeferral=false (control case)
  intent: null
  skipDeferral present: false

=== Results ===
Timer path: skipDeferral=true produces intent with skipDeferral, drain timeout -> 0
Active-deferral bypass: skipDeferral=true now threads intent (was missing before fix)
Normal restart: no skipDeferral in intent, full drain budget applies
Both code paths verified: SIGUSR1 handler receives skipDeferral for skip-deferral restarts
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production code at `dist/restart-D_lgNyR8.js:624` and `:673` correctly threads `skipDeferral: true` through the restart intent in both the active-deferral bypass path and the timer path. The SIGUSR1 handler at `dist/run-COQAnaaw.js:385` returns drain timeout 0 when `skipDeferral` is present, bypassing the pending reply drain wait. Runtime exercise confirms the production `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart` produces intent `{"skipDeferral":true,"reason":"gateway.restart.safe"}` through both paths. Without `skipDeferral`, the intent is `null` and the full drain budget applies.

What was not tested: Live `--safe --skip-deferral` restart with active pending replies from a channel-originated turn. The fix is verified at the intent-threading and timeout-resolution layers; end-to-end restart with live channel delivery requires configured channel credentials.

target.title: fix: relay Claude CLI assistant partial messages as streaming deltas
target.body:
Fixes #86050

## Summary

Relay Claude CLI assistant records as streaming deltas so the WebChat UI shows progressive output instead of waiting for the full response.

**Fix 1**: Parse `assistant` records from the Claude CLI JSONL stream and forward their text content as `chat.delta` events.

**Fix 2**: Gate on `stop_reason` using a positive partial signal: only forward records where `stop_reason` is explicitly present and `null` (the documented Claude CLI partial marker). Complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped to avoid duplicating the final message. Records without `stop_reason` at all are ignored as ambiguous (could be complete messages from custom `claude-stream-json` backends).

## Changed files

- `src/agents/cli-output.ts` -- Added `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` with positive `stop_reason: null` guard
- `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` -- Tests covering partial forwarding, complete-message skip, absent-stop_reason rejection, and edge cases

## Verification

All tests use the documented `stop_reason: null` marker for partial messages:
- `stop_reason: null` (explicit) -> text forwarded as streaming delta
- `stop_reason: "end_turn"` -> record skipped (complete message, not a partial)
- `stop_reason` absent -> record skipped (not a confirmed partial; prevents false delivery from custom backends)
- Non-assistant record types -> ignored by the parser
- Malformed content arrays -> returns null without throwing
- Mixed text + tool_use content -> text extracted, tool_use entries ignored

Fixes #86050

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude CLI assistant records are not relayed as streaming deltas, so WebChat waits for the full response. The `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function requires a positive partial signal (`stop_reason: null` explicitly present) to skip complete messages and forward only true streaming partials as `chat.delta` events.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from branch `fix/gateway-stream-relay` at `/tmp/proof-86649`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify the partial assistant delta parser is active in compiled production code.
Step 2. Feed four Claude CLI JSONL records through the production `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`: two partials (`stop_reason: null`), one complete (`stop_reason: "end_turn"`), and one ambiguous (no `stop_reason` field).

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The partial assistant delta parser and its `stop_reason: null` guard are active in the compiled production bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta\|stop_reason.*null\|partialDelta" dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js
216:function parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta(params) {
220:if (!isRecord$1(message) || !("stop_reason" in message) || message.stop_reason !== null) return null;
394:const partialDelta = parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta({
402:if (partialDelta) {
403:assistantText = partialDelta.text;
404:params.onAssistantDelta(partialDelta);
```

Runtime exercise of the production parser with Claude CLI JSONL event shapes:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-exercise.ts
=== Feeding Claude CLI JSONL records through production parser ===

Input record 1 (partial, stop_reason: null):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"}]

Input record 2 (partial, stop_reason: null, extended text):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer"}],"stop_reason":null},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]

Input record 3 (complete, stop_reason: "end_turn"):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Here is the answer to your question."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Complete message skipped (no new delta), avoiding duplicate delivery

Input record 4 (no stop_reason field, ambiguous source):
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Some response"}]},"session_id":"sess-abc123"}
  -> Deltas so far: [{"text":"Here is","delta":"Here is"},{"text":"Here is the answer","delta":" the answer"}]
  -> Record without stop_reason skipped (not a confirmed partial)

=== Summary ===
Total records fed: 4
Partial deltas relayed: 2
Delta breakdown:
  [0] delta="Here is" (cumulative: "Here is")
  [1] delta=" the answer" (cumulative: "Here is the answer")

Before fix: Records 1-2 would not emit deltas (assistant type was ignored).
After fix: Records 1-2 relay as streaming deltas. Records 3-4 correctly skipped.
No duplicate final text delivered.
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production code at `dist/claude-live-session-BYm1bWnI.js:216-220` contains the `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` function with the positive partial signal guard (`stop_reason !== null`). At line 394, the main parser calls this function as a fallback when no raw stream delta is available. Runtime exercise confirms: assistant records with `stop_reason: null` relay as incremental streaming deltas (`"Here is"` then `" the answer"`), complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped (preventing duplicate delivery), and ambiguous records without `stop_reason` are also skipped (preventing false delivery from custom backends).

What was not tested: Live Claude CLI session with real streaming output over the gateway WebSocket connection. The fix exercises the production parser against the documented Claude CLI JSONL protocol shapes. End-to-end WebChat/TUI display was not verified.

target.title: Fix stale cron catch-up after schedule updates
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91944.

Cron restart catch-up can infer a missed cron slot from `lastRunAtMs` even when `nextRunAtMs` already points to the future. That catch-up path is valid for ordinary restart recovery, but after `cron.update` changes scheduling inputs it can replay an old slot that predates the new schedule's activation.

This change records when schedule/enabled inputs are updated and makes startup catch-up skip inferred cron slots older than that activation time. The focused regression covers the reported monthly schedule shape, an isolated `agentTurn`, and the 120s startup deferral path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a cron job updated from a day-10 monthly schedule to a day-11 monthly schedule no longer gets treated as a missed day-11 slot from the previous month and deferred to `gateway startup + 120s` on restart.

Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout at `26b5b5efb9` with Node 24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2. The runtime proof used an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and OpenClaw's real SQLite cron store path, then imported the source `CronService`, `saveCronStore`, and `loadCronStore` modules directly from this PR head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the repository root with a stdin script that seeded the SQLite-backed cron store, called `CronService.update("monthly-agent-report", { schedule: { kind: "cron", expr: "18 15 11 * *", tz: "UTC" } })` at `2026-06-09T22:30:00.000Z`, then started a fresh `CronService` at `2026-06-10T20:33:00.000Z` with `startupDeferredMissedAgentJobDelayMs: 120000`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts --testNamePattern "does not defer a cron slot that predates a schedule update|replays the most recent missed cron slot|defers overdue isolated"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service.issue-regressions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the runtime proof script on PR head `26b5b5efb9`:

```json
{
  "proof": "#91944 cron schedule-update restart catch-up",
  "sourceCheckout": "/Users/andy/openclaw-91944-cron-migration",
  "stateRoot": "/var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-91944-proof-9sMwyj",
  "sqlitePath": "/var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-91944-proof-9sMwyj/state/openclaw.sqlite",
  "scenario": {
    "oldSchedule": "18 15 10 * * UTC",
    "newSchedule": "18 15 11 * * UTC",
    "updateNow": "2026-06-09T22:30:00.000Z",
    "restartNow": "2026-06-10T20:33:00.000Z",
    "legacyCandidateSlot": "2026-05-11T15:18:00.000Z",
    "startupDeferralTarget": "2026-06-10T20:35:00.000Z"
  },
  "afterCronUpdate": {
    "persistedSchedule": {
      "kind": "cron",
      "expr": "18 15 11 * *",
      "tz": "UTC"
    },
    "scheduleUpdatedAtMs": "2026-06-09T22:30:00.000Z",
    "nextRunAtMs": "2026-06-11T15:18:00.000Z"
  },
  "afterRestart": {
    "nextRunAtMs": "2026-06-11T15:18:00.000Z",
    "lastRunAtMs": "2026-05-10T15:18:00.000Z",
    "lastRunStatus": "ok",
    "isolatedAgentRuns": 0,
    "systemEvents": 0,
    "heartbeats": 0,
    "persistedNextRunAtMs": "2026-06-11T15:18:00.000Z"
  },
  "result": "PASS: restart did not defer or replay the pre-update cron slot; next run stayed on 2026-06-11T15:18:00.000Z"
}
```

Observed result after fix: after the schedule update, `nextRunAtMs` is `2026-06-11T15:18:00.000Z` and `scheduleUpdatedAtMs` is `2026-06-09T22:30:00.000Z`. Starting the cron service during the reported June 10 restart window leaves `nextRunAtMs` persisted at June 11, keeps `lastRunAtMs` at the real May 10 run, and makes zero isolated-agent, system-event, or heartbeat calls.

What was not tested: no live gateway upgrade, launchd double-restart, or Telegram delivery was run. The live proof is local source-runtime behavior through OpenClaw's cron service and SQLite state store for the scheduler state sequence from the issue.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts` - 15 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts` - 25 passed

target.title: feat(msteams): Teams voice (CVI) + chat + governance integration
target.body:
> **Stacked on #91438 — review the increment** (`5249ad9bbb…ac682d0ac2`, 30 commits): the foundation (`16e83178bc…2110577317`) is #91438. The full diff includes #91438's commits only until that PR lands; this branch then rebases to exactly the increment. A commit→feature map is below.

## Summary

Builds on the `msteams` voice provider (#91438) along five lines:

- **Correctness / security** — fixes the realtime **self-answer (echo) bug**, makes the bridge handshake **replay-proof**, enforces group etiquette and **verbal interrupts deterministically** (in code at the audio egress, not model-trusted), and fixes outbound call-backs that **spoke to a still-ringing phone** (and the context-bleed that caused).
- **Call UX** — verbal interrupts ("stop" / wake-phrase forms), notify call-backs (deliver → hang up, un-clipped), **greet-by-name** from the roster, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** ("press 1 to…").
- **CVI** — retroactive vision ("what did the earlier slide say?"), scene-change + camera/screen ambient vision, captioned/slideshow visual sharing as a **picture-in-picture overlay** over the avatar, a **"thinking"** expression, per-thread sessions.
- **Meeting productivity** — opt-in **end-of-meeting minutes** posted to the Teams chat/thread, on-demand **"summarize the meeting"** mid-call, a **Word-openable minutes document**, and **per-person attribution** from unmixed meeting audio.
- **Chat** — a Teams **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** routes a selected chat message to the agent.

| Layer | Themes |
| --- | --- |
| **Correctness / security** | echo self-answer fix (`suppressInputDuringPlayback` → on), replay-proof HMAC tuples, deterministic group gate on realtime, playout clock for echo + notify drain, **greet-on-answer** (outbound call-backs no longer deliver to a ringing phone) |
| **Call UX** | verbal interrupts, notify call-backs (deliver → hang up), **roster greeting by name**, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** |
| **CVI** | retroactive vision (16-keyframe ring), scene-change + camera/screen vision, captions + slideshow, **PiP overlay**, "thinking" expression, per-thread sessions |
| **Meeting productivity** | end-of-meeting recap to chat/thread, on-demand `/summarize`, DOCX minutes artifact, per-speaker attribution (unmixed audio) |
| **Chat** | "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action |

## ⚠️ Behavior changes

- `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default **flipped off → on**: default-off *was* the live self-answer bug — the bot answered its own echo on every realtime deployment. Set `false` to restore; tune via `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `realtime.echoBargeInRms`.
- Outbound realtime call-backs now honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify** = state the result, then hang up after the audio drains) **and speak only once the callee answers** (recording-active), not when the bridge connects. Previously they greeted on connect — i.e. while the phone was still ringing — so the result was lost and the idle model could surface unrelated context. Set `"conversation"` to keep the lingering behavior.
- `msteams.meetingRecap` (default **off**) — when on, the bot posts meeting minutes to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or the meeting thread (group) at call end. The bot posting unprompted is gated behind this opt-in.
- `msteams.bilingual` (default **off**) — when on, the realtime model is pinned to detect/mirror Arabic↔English and translate on request.

## 🛡️ Security & correctness hardening

- **Single-use HMAC upgrade tuples (replay guard)** — a captured handshake replayed within the HMAC window could open a ghost session after the live call ended. `(callId, timestamp, signature)` tuples are single-use; a legitimate reconnect carries a fresh timestamp; only verified tuples are recorded (no unauthenticated map growth) and entries expire with the window. Tested: connect OK → exact replay 401 → fresh-timestamp reconnect OK.
- **Deterministic group-call gate (realtime)** — every finished caller turn is evaluated with the same `shouldRespondToGroupTurn` core the streaming path uses; an unaddressed meeting turn's reply **audio is dropped at the egress**. 1:1 never gates.
- **Echo guard on a playout clock** — the realtime model streams faster than realtime, so last-send time is not play-time; the guard and the notify drain both key on a playout estimate (accumulated chunk durations, collapsed on barge-in). Regression test: burst 10 s of audio → echo still suppressed at +2 s.
- **Greet-on-answer (outbound call-backs)** — the greeting (the delivered result) now fires on recording-active (the callee answered), not on bridge-connect; this both delivers the result at the right time and closes the idle window where the model would otherwise free-associate from its context.

## 📞 Call experience

- **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop" / "wait" / "hold on" / "never mind" handled in code: streaming cuts playback **and suppresses the reply**; realtime flushes the worker queue. Whole-utterance match with filler **and configured wake-phrase stripping** — *"⟨name⟩, stop"* cuts as instantly as bare *"stop"*; *"stop by the store"* never triggers.
- **Roster greeting** — the model greets the caller by first name (from the Teams roster) and addresses participants by the per-utterance speaker label.
- **Bilingual Arabic/English** (opt-in) — detect, mirror, switch-with-caller, translate on request.
- **DTMF / IVR** — in-band keypad tones from the caller are surfaced to the realtime model as a user message ("the caller pressed 1"), so the agent can run "press 1 to…" flows. *(Worker companion forwards the tone.)*
- **Notify call-backs deliver-and-hang-up without clipping** — drain-aware (playout clock) + `notifyHangupDelaySec` grace; a failed hangup falls back to a direct teardown.

## 🎥 CVI extensions

- **Retroactive vision (frame history)** — a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, attributed keyframes to **one budgeted consult**. No eager OCR-per-keyframe.
- **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** — frames reach the realtime model on arrival; **camera + screen simultaneously**, per-source dedup + labels.
- **`show_to_caller` captions + slideshow + PiP overlay** — caption strip on the tile; multi-image results play as a paced slideshow; images ride as a **bottom-right inset over the live avatar** (`display.image mode:"overlay"`) so the bot stays visible. Additive protocol; older renderers fall back to fullscreen.
- **"Thinking" avatar expression** while a tool runs; the reply re-cues the real emotion.
- **Per-thread sessions** — `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys the agent session by the Teams meeting/chat thread; parallel conversations stay isolated. PSTN falls through to per-phone.

## 📝 Meeting productivity

- **End-of-meeting recap** (opt-in `msteams.meetingRecap`) — key points / decisions / action items posted to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or **into the meeting thread** (group) when the call ends.
- **On-demand minutes** — "summarize the meeting" mid-call posts the running minutes to chat without ending the call (`post_meeting_minutes` tool).
- **Word-openable minutes artifact** — the recap attaches a formatted document the caller can open in Word.
- **Per-person attribution** — with unmixed meeting audio the worker tags each turn with the speaker, so minutes and transcripts read "Sara: …" instead of one mixed stream.

## 💬 Chat

- **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** — a Teams message-context command (`composeExtension/submitAction`) quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path, so the answer posts back into the conversation. *(Requires the Teams app manifest to declare the command: `composeExtensions > commands`, `type: "action"`, `context: ["message"]`, `id: "askOpenClaw"`.)*

## Commit → feature map (increment beyond #91438)

| Feature | Commits |
| --- | --- |
| Echo / self-answer fix (+ playout clock, streaming, knobs) | `cc9f7302b3`, `64aca022c3`, `514dffba49` |
| Replay guard | `1bd04c1417` |
| Deterministic group gate | `6676bdf3da` |
| Verbal interrupts (+ wake-phrase form) | `5c827481f7`, `2110577317` |
| Retroactive vision | `0af72f4f8e`, `6676bdf3da` |
| Per-thread sessions | `d1733c2d95` |
| Captions + slideshow | `f4565c05d5`, `72fd39cec7` |
| Notify call-backs (mode + drain + greet-on-answer) | `41c2d3c11e`, `0b28bf2d0b`, `7bb86549ff` |
| Scene-change + camera/screen vision | `7cf6b04582`, `5b7f3db8af` |
| "Thinking" expression | `d5c2a0fa76` |
| Meeting recap + thread + on-demand + DOCX + attribution | `99e7a1bacb`, `2c699c03b1`, `4c35262059`, `70a73a6405`, `31be414b59`, `a7452c21e2` |
| PiP overlay (#17) | `b632b7f9ad` |
| Roster greeting (#20) | `fc12d3028a` |
| Bilingual (#19) | `672f34ec51` |
| DTMF / IVR (#21) | `e20c5e1f59` |
| "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action (#10) | `ac682d0ac2` |
| Refactors (vision store, tts playback) | `5249ad9bbb`, `7654aaacb5` |

Each feature ships with unit tests (matcher/positive-negative units, display-path, history-ring, group-gate egress drop, replay 401/reconnect, recap prompt, DTMF mapping, message-action extraction, etc.).

## Foundation recap (from #91438, included in this diff until it lands)

The `msteams` provider: inbound Teams voice + video vision (`look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, realtime continuous vision via `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage`), group/meeting awareness (per-participant attribution + speak-only-when-addressed), outbound "call me back" with voicemail fallback, avatar rendering drivers (expression cues, viseme lip-shape, `show_to_caller`), caller allowlist (closed by default), recording-status gate on every media-derived path, HMAC-authenticated bridge WebSocket, SSRF-guarded outbound. Live-proven on 1:1 and 3-party group Teams calls.

## Notes
- Several features (DTMF tone capture, unmixed meeting audio, avatar rendering) have a companion in the application-hosted media worker; the protocol additions are backward-compatible (older workers ignore unknown fields).
- The recap/minutes and bilingual features are **opt-in**; defaults preserve prior behavior except the two flagged behavior changes above (echo guard, greet-on-answer).


## Acknowledgements

Contributed by the team at the Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC) — Dubai, United Arab Emirates — bringing Microsoft Teams voice & video (the Conversational Video Interface) to OpenClaw for public-sector use. We're glad to share it upstream and welcome the maintainers' guidance on shaping it for the wider community.

target.title: fix(gateway): mirror hidden channel assistant commentary
target.body:
## Problem

Channel-originated sessions can subscribe to session-message events without the Control UI being visible. That hidden-session path already forwards tool events, but assistant commentary frames were not mirrored to those session-message subscribers.

ClickClack depends on those hidden session-message events to build its preamble. The result was asymmetric live behavior:

- tool calls flowed into ClickClack
- assistant commentary did not
- the final answer still arrived, so the failure looked like a missing preamble rather than a broken run

This is separate from #92092. That PR fixed claude-cli narration being dropped before gateway delivery. This PR fixes the gateway fanout path for hidden/channel sessions after assistant commentary already exists as an agent event.

## Root cause

`createAgentEventHandler()` had two distinct fanout behaviors:

1. Control-UI-visible runs: assistant events are sent through the normal visible UI agent-event path.
2. Non-Control-UI-visible runs: selected session-message subscribers get hidden live chat/tool updates, but assistant commentary events fall through and are not sent to those exact subscribers.

The hidden path was therefore complete for tool activity but incomplete for assistant commentary.

## Fix

Add a narrow hidden-session mirror path for assistant commentary events:

- only runs when `isControlUiVisible` is false
- only sends to exact session-message subscribers for the resolved session key
- only forwards assistant events that are commentary or streaming assistant deltas
- explicitly excludes `final_answer` so final replies are not double-posted
- preserves abort suppression
- uses the existing hidden `sendAgentPayload(..., { controlUiVisible: false, dropIfSlow: true })` route

## Contract documentation

This PR also adds `docs/channels/agent-event-io-contract.md`, a provider-input and channel-output contract for this boundary. It documents the normalized `assistant`, `tool`, `item`, and `lifecycle` expectations, explicitly covers delta-only assistant frames, hidden channel-session mirroring, final-answer exclusion, and channel projection into preamble/tool activity rows.

The contract is included here because the ClickClack failure exposed an undocumented seam: provider/runtime events existed, but hidden channel subscribers did not receive the assistant-commentary subset. Future provider or channel work should not have to rediscover that mapping.

## Tests

Focused gateway regression coverage in `src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`:

- commentary with `phase: "commentary"` is mirrored to exact hidden session subscribers
- assistant streaming delta without an explicit phase is mirrored
- assistant text without a delta is not mirrored
- `phase: "final_answer"` is not mirrored
- hidden commentary is not globally broadcast
- hidden commentary is not sent via `nodeSendToSession`
- other session subscribers do not receive the event
- aborted hidden runs do not mirror commentary

Validation run:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts --maxWorkers=1

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       87 passed (87)
```

Additional checks:

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core
# clean

$ pnpm tsgo:test:src
# clean

$ git diff --check
# clean

$ pnpm docs:check-mdx
# Docs MDX check passed

$ pnpm lint:docs
# markdownlint passed
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: hidden/channel session subscribers received tool events but not assistant commentary events, preventing ClickClack from building commentary preambles for channel-originated user turns.

- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway built from this patch and restarted on `2026-06-11T15:31Z`; ClickClack bridge subscribed to the real channel session `agent:chisel:clickclack:channel:channel:chn_01ktq4zg9bga4dqrycqz67jts6`; real ClickClack `#test` and `#general` turns driven by ragesaq through the deployed bridge.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  1. Built the OpenClaw gateway from branch `fix/channel-hidden-commentary`.
  2. Restarted `openclaw-gateway.service` and `clickclack-bridge.service`.
  3. Drove channel-originated ClickClack turns from ragesaq in `#test` and `#general`.
  4. Confirmed ClickClack persisted `agent_commentary` rows alongside `agent_tool` rows after deploy.
  5. Captured before/after ClickClack screenshots from the real user-facing channel UI.

- Evidence after fix:

  Before this patch, ragesaq's channel-originated ClickClack turn reached the final answer, but no commentary/tool preamble rendered for the long-running turn:

  ![before: final answer without commentary preamble](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/before-real-user-channel-proof.png)

  After this patch, ragesaq's channel-originated ClickClack turn renders an expanded preamble with assistant commentary interleaved with tool-call rows:

  ![after: expanded preamble with commentary and tool calls](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/after-real-user-channel-proof.png)

  ClickClack also persisted real `agent_commentary` rows in the channel alongside `agent_tool` rows. Example rows from the ClickClack DB after deploy:

```text
2026-06-11T15:55:29.371130227Z  agent_commentary  Pushed (`6055c05`). Posting the summary to the channel.
2026-06-11T15:55:21.602563769Z  agent_tool        **exec**
2026-06-11T15:55:41.92467386Z   agent_tool        **message**
```

  Additional user-visible proof screenshots are committed on fork branch `proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence`:

  - expanded preamble, commentary interleaved with tool-call rows: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/preamble-expanded-commentary-tools.png
  - collapsed preamble with final answer still visible: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/preamble-collapsed-final-visible.png
  - proof note: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ragesaq/openclaw/proof/channel-hidden-commentary-evidence/.proof/channel-hidden-commentary/real-behavior-proof.md

- Observed result after fix: ClickClack receives hidden assistant commentary frames for the exact channel session, persists them as durable `agent_commentary` messages, and renders a user-visible preamble containing commentary plus tool-call rows while the final answer remains visible.

- What was not tested: upstream-maintainer-hosted deployment. This PR also does not change or test ClickClack rendering internals; it fixes the OpenClaw gateway fanout path that ClickClack already consumes.

## Risk checklist

- **Behavior change scope:** hidden/channel session-message subscribers now receive assistant commentary events they were already subscribed to but missing. Control-UI-visible behavior is untouched.
- **Final-answer duplication:** explicitly guarded by excluding `phase === "final_answer"`.
- **Session isolation:** uses the existing exact session-message subscriber registry and test asserts only the selected session receives events.
- **Abort behavior:** preserved and covered by regression test.
- **Persistence/schema:** no schema or storage changes.
- **Backpressure:** uses the existing hidden subscriber `dropIfSlow: true` behavior.

## Not in scope

- ClickClack rendering implementation. This PR fixes the OpenClaw gateway event fanout path only.
- #92092's claude-cli parser lane fix. That bug was upstream of this path and has already merged.

## AI assistance

AI-assisted. Authored under ragesaq's OpenClaw contributor identity with Forge and Chisel assistance. ragesaq supplied the real before/after ClickClack screenshots and directed the proof update; Chisel prepared this PR body/proof revision with `openai/gpt-5.5`.

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent outbound A2A allowlist override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `agents.list[].tools.agentToAgent.allow` to control which agents a specific agent may contact via session tools (`sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `session_status`)
- When unset, the agent falls back to the global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` list; when set to explicit IDs, only those targets are reachable; `[]` isolates the agent from all cross-agent access
- The global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` continues to act as a participation gate (both requester and target must match it) before per-agent outbound rules are evaluated
- Outbound-only semantics: only the requester's override is checked, not the target's
- Replace `matchesAllow` with `matchesGlobalParticipation` and add `evaluateAccess()` returning a structured `AgentToAgentDenyReason` so all call sites produce context-aware error messages via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`
- Scrub deleted agent references from per-agent outbound allowlists in `pruneAgentConfig()`

### Example config (hub-spoke pattern)

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "david",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["*"] } } },
      { "id": "spoke",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } },
      { "id": "kelly",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } }
    ]
  }
}
```

spoke→david ✅ | spoke→kelly ❌ | david→anyone ✅

## Test plan

- [x] `sessions-access.test.ts`: per-agent override, deny-all `[]`, global gate, target override ignored (outbound-only), fallback to global when unset
- [x] `sessions.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny during label lookup in `sessions_send`
- [x] `openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny for cross-agent `session_status`
- [x] `agents.test.ts`: `pruneAgentConfig` scrubs deleted agent from per-agent allowlists
- [x] Build passes
- [x] Manual: configure hub-spoke agents (david/spoke/kelly), verify per-agent outbound rules — see `Real behavior proof` section below

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: per-agent outbound `agentToAgent.allow` correctly gates cross-agent session tool access. Hub-spoke topology used: `david` (hub, `allow=["*"]`), `spoke` (allow=`["david"]`), `kelly` (allow=`["david"]`). Validates the new `evaluateAccess()` decision pipeline reaches the documented per-agent outbound semantics, with the structured `per_agent_outbound` deny reason surfaced via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`.

- **Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 22.04 (kernel 5.15), Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10. Branch HEAD `55819f6f43` of `repair/pr-39102-a2a-refresh` (this PR rebased on current main). Live invocation of the production export `createAgentToAgentPolicy` from `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — no mocks, no test runner, the same code path that backs `sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `session_status` at runtime.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  1. Author a hub-spoke `OpenClawConfig` matching the docs example (david hub + spoke + kelly).
  2. Build `policy = createAgentToAgentPolicy(cfg)`.
  3. Call `policy.evaluateAccess(requester, target)` for the five edge pairs.
  4. Run with: `pnpm tsx proof-hub-spoke.ts` (the script imports the worktree source directly so the running code is the patched export).

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied verbatim from `node` stdout via `tsx`.

  ```
  # per-agent A2A allowlist — hub-spoke proof
  # config: david=hub (allow *), spoke=spoke (allow david), kelly=spoke (allow david)

  [ok ] spoke -> david: ALLOWED
  [ok ] spoke -> kelly: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)
  [ok ] david -> kelly: ALLOWED
  [ok ] david -> spoke: ALLOWED
  [ok ] kelly -> spoke: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)

  all cases match expected behavior
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: all five real invocations matched the documented hub-spoke semantics. Cross-spoke calls (`spoke -> kelly`, `kelly -> spoke`) were denied with the structured `per_agent_outbound` reason code from `AgentToAgentDecision`. Hub-rooted calls (`david -> spoke`, `david -> kelly`) and spoke-to-hub (`spoke -> david`) were allowed. Process exit code: `0`. Console output above is the literal stdout, no post-processing.

- **What was not tested**: end-to-end IPC roundtrip through the live `sessions_send` agent process pair (single-machine ACP transport). The four session call sites all delegate the access decision to the single `evaluateAccess()` policy proven above, and the wiring of each call site is covered by the unit tests already listed in the Test plan.

target.title: Gateway: allow extension origins in browser allowlist
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` could not explicitly allowlist `chrome-extension://` because the origin checker normalized extension origins through `URL.origin`, which becomes `"null"` for that scheme. - Why it matters: extension sidepanel clients had to rely on `["*"]` to get through browser-origin WS checks, which weakened the intended hardening. - What changed: the origin matcher now keeps exact URL parsing/validation but falls back to exact `scheme://host` matching for non-standard origins, so `chrome-extension://` can be allowlisted exactly; focused unit and handshake tests were added. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no wildcard/prefix expansion, no acceptance of literal `Origin: null`, no changes to token/device auth, host-header fallback, or non-browser client auth behavior. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #35035 (issue mentions app://* origin, but it's the same underlying problem) - Related #35396, #35057 (both PRS were fixes for this problem but were closed because the authors were banned AFAIK) ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` can now contain exact extension origins such as `chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop`, and those browser-origin Gateway WS connects will succeed when otherwise authenticated. - Operators no longer need `["*"]` solely to let a Chrome extension sidepanel client connect. - Existing exact matches for normal `http(s)` origins, wildcard behavior, host-header fallback, and literal `Origin: null` rejection remain unchanged. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` (strictly speaking this allows chrome extensions/electron+tauri apps to connect but I think calling it a new capability is a stretch) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `N/A` ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local Bun + Vitest - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WS browser-origin client; Chrome extension origin emulated in handshake tests via `ws` - Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.auth.mode="token"`, `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]` ### Steps 1. Configure the Gateway with token auth and `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop"]`. 2. Open a WS connection that sends `Origin: chrome-extension://abcdefghijklmnop` and a valid `connect` request. 3. Observe the handshake result. ### Expected - An exactly allowlisted extension origin should be accepted, just like an exactly allowlisted `https://...` origin. ### Actual - Before this fix, the request was rejected with `origin not allowed` because the checker collapsed the extension origin to `"null"` internally. - After this fix, the handshake succeeds and returns `hello-ok`. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: whitelisted the chrome extension I'm developing and successfully connected to the Gateway via WS - Edge cases checked: N/A - What you did **not** verify: did not try with other protocols (`app://`, `tauri://`) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: `N/A` ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this commit; if extension access is urgently blocked, temporarily fall back to `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins=["*"]` until the regression is understood. - Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/origin-check.ts`; optionally restore prior `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` values in config. - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: an exactly allowlisted `chrome-extension://` still being rejected; malformed/non-matching non-standard origins unexpectedly being accepted; regressions in existing `http(s)` origin allowlisting.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

## Fix

1. **model-fallback.ts**: Add an explicit guard **before** `coerceToFailoverError` in `runFallbackCandidate`: if the error is an `AbortError` and the external abort signal is already aborted, rethrow immediately (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s (no external signal) fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before coerceToFailoverError normalization:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
  throw err;
}
```

2. **compact.ts**: Thread `params.abortSignal` into the `runWithModelFallback` call so compaction cancellations are correctly forwarded to the new guard.

Also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **3 files changed**, +66 / -8
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard moved before normalization (core fix)
- `compact.ts`: abortSignal forwarding (P2 fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: 3 regression tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Provider-side `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` (no external abort signal) was misclassified by `shouldRethrowAbort()` → `isTimeoutError()` matching the message, causing the fallback loop to silently swallow the error. In group/channel sessions this produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`, permanently silencing the topic with zero visible response.

- **Real environment tested**: macOS (Apple Silicon), Node v22, patched OpenClaw dist at `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/model-fallback--w47Y_pS.js`. Gateway restarted with patched dist. Guard logic verified against the actual production module code.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Patch the dist file with the fix
# 2. Restart the gateway
```
